2026-05-03T20:12:11Z

## T004 Manifest Snapshot (NFR-002 baseline)
Captured: 2026-05-03T20:12:11Z

## T005 Internal-Tooling Search (Q-1 Adjudication)
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Stream 1 Day 1)
Paths searched: .aod/scripts/, .claude/agents/, .claude/commands/, Makefile, scripts/
Match count: 0 (zero) in every required path
Broader sanity check (*.sh, *.md, *.py, *.json, *.yml, *.yaml, Makefile excluding .git/, node_modules/, specs/, docs/): 0 consumer references outside the file itself
Adjudicated disposition: Option (b) — remove .claude/mcp-config.json (T033)
Fallback Option (a) NOT triggered. Canonical placeholder set remains locked at 12.

## T008 Stream 1 Day 1 Baseline Benchmark (NFR-004 anchor)
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Stream 1 Day 1 pre-swap)
Snapshot source: tmpdir clone of branch=main, HEAD=da42235 (latest commit before 248-substitution-surface-hardening branch)
Inputs (canonical fixture, stdin-fed):
  PROJECT_NAME=tachi
  PROJECT_DESCRIPTION=threat modeling sidecar
  GITHUB_ORG=benchmark-test-org
  GITHUB_REPO=(empty → defaults to tachi)
  AI_AGENT=Claude Code (choice 1)
  Stack pack=Other (choice 6 of 6 — 5 packs detected + Other)
  TECH_STACK=Python + FastAPI
  TECH_STACK_DATABASE=PostgreSQL
  CLOUD_PROVIDER=(empty → "Not yet defined")
  Confirm=Y
Environment: LC_ALL=C, HOME=isolated tmpdir (gh auth state neutralized), bash 3.2.57 (macOS default)
init.sh exit: 0
Timing (bash time builtin):
  real    0m6.690s
  user    0m5.326s
  sys     0m0.926s

Note: this baseline captures the full init.sh flow including:
- prompts (stdin instant), prerequisite checks, AOD_REPO write, vision seeding,
- BOARD_STATUS gh detection (skipped_no_gh because HOME=isolated),
- replace_in_files() sed-based substitution loop (Linux-or-macOS branch),
- constitution sed cleanup at lines 235-241,
- version pin write, personalization snapshot write, self-delete.

The substitution loop itself is sed-based on macOS via `find ... -exec sed -i '' ...`.
T021 (post-Stream-1) will measure the same flow with bash parameter expansion via
aod_template_substitute_placeholders. Delta thresholds per NFR-004:
  ≤ 5% : holds at 10% (record numbers in ADR-038)
  5–50%: loosen to 25% with rationale (ADR-038 §Consequences)
  > 50%: PM escalation pre-merge

## Wave 2 — Test Authorship (T009-T013) — TEST-FIRST per Constitution VI
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Day 1 EOD per accelerated /aod.build run)
Deliverables landed:
  tests/scripts/init_sh_helpers.py             (shared helper module, ~210 LOC)
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_substitution.py   (Test-1, 2 tests; skip until baseline regenerated post-Stream-1 / T014)
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_adversarial.py    (Test-2, 14 tests = 12 parametrized + case-13 + residual scan)
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_constitution.py   (Test-4, 1 test)
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_self_delete.py    (Test-5', 3 tests)
  tests/fixtures/regenerate-baseline.sh        (T013 regen script, executable)

pytest --collect-only: 20 tests collected, 0 errors
NFR-003 (>=13 adversarial input minimum): SATISFIED (12 parametrized + case-13 = 13 cases)

G2 quality gate (TEST-FIRST per Constitution VI):
  - All 5 test files committed BEFORE Wave 3 implementation
  - Tests assert post-F-1 contracts (will fail pre-Stream-1, pass post-Stream-1+T014)
  - Pre-merge baseline FAIL expected => tests will be exercised at start of Wave 3 to confirm

Helper architecture:
  - clone_into_tmpdir(): clones REPO_ROOT @ HEAD via file:// + git checkout (reproducible; sees feature branch commits as Wave 3 lands)
  - run_init_in_clone(): LC_ALL=C + isolated HOME (gh auth neutralized) + PATH minus homebrew dirs
  - build_canonical_stdin(): pack-count-aware "Other" choice; matches T008 baseline inputs
  - files_in_tree(): mirrors init.sh find filter (excludes .git/, node_modules/, *.png/jpg/ico)


## Wave 3 G2 Pre-Check — Test Collection Defect + Expected Failure Pattern
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Wave 3 entry)

### Test infra defect (FIXED in this run)
- pytest from project root: `python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_init_sh_*.py` failed with
  4 ERRORS in collection: `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'init_sh_helpers'`
- Root cause: `tests/scripts/__init__.py` makes the directory a Python package, so
  `from init_sh_helpers import ...` (top-level import in 4 test files) cannot resolve
  unless `tests/scripts/` is on sys.path. The Wave 2 runlog claim "20 tests collected,
  0 errors" was true ONLY when running `cd tests/scripts && pytest ...` — the directory
  becomes its own working dir, satisfying the implicit imports. Project-root execution
  (T039 protocol) failed.
- Fix applied: added 2-line sys.path.insert to `tests/scripts/conftest.py` so the
  helpers directory resolves regardless of cwd. Surgical fix; no test files modified.
  Tests now collect from project root: 20 tests collected, 0 errors.

### G2 expected-failure pattern (CONFIRMED)
Run from project root, post-Wave-2 / pre-Stream-1 baseline:
  - 8 passed (Test-2 cases 1, 4, 5, 6, 8 substituted; Test-5' self-delete + snapshot persists + version persists)
  - 10 failed (Test-2 cases 2,3,7,8 substituted [sed metachar corruption]; Test-2 cases 9-12 rejected [no input validator yet]; Test-2 case 13 + residual scan; Test-4 constitution byte-compare)
  - 2 skipped (Test-1 fixture-replay — baseline not regenerated until T014)
  - Total runtime: 231.45s (3:51 — heavy due to per-test full init.sh subprocess run)
- Match against NEXT-SESSION.md predicted pattern: 9/10 expected, 1 unanticipated (case-7+8 in
  substitution failed pre-Stream-1; predicted only cases 1-6 would fail). Test corpus is
  asserting correct invariants; G2 confirmed.

## T021 Stream 1 Day 1 Post-Swap Benchmark — BLOCKED
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Wave 3 in-flight)

**Status**: BLOCKED — NFR-004 ceiling exceeded AND residual scan over-scoping defect.
**Disposition pending**: PM re-scope (perf threshold breach) + Architect review
(residual-scan regex scope vs. canonical-12 lockstep contract per ADR-038).

### Run 1: pre-fix benchmark
Snapshot source: tmpdir clone of branch=248-substitution-surface-hardening,
HEAD=4ace34a (T015-T020 committed; T021 not yet)
Inputs: same canonical fixture as T008 baseline
Environment: HOME=isolated tmpdir (gh auth neutralized), LC_ALL=C, full PATH
(homebrew kept because node-on-/opt/homebrew/bin needed; `_safe_path` fallback
documented in init_sh_helpers.py:159-176)
Outcome: STALLED — substitution loop exceeded 4 minutes wall-clock, killed by
operator. Cause: bash parameter expansion `${content//pattern/replacement}` on
multi-MB binary files (PDFs at 7MB, 6.7MB, 6.3MB, 2.2MB, ...) triggers O(file_size
× 12 keys) string-scan operation that does not progress on large binaries.
Mitigation 1: extended init.sh find filter to skip *.pdf, *.baseline (PDF baselines),
.DS_Store. These files have no `{{KEY}}` placeholders by design — original sed
processed them harmlessly because sed line-streams binaries; bash param expansion
loads entire content into a shell variable.

### Run 2: post-binary-skip benchmark
HEAD=40e41b6 (find filter extended)
Outcome: real 49.928s (vs T008 baseline 6.690s = +646% delta), exit 8 due to
residual scan halt at .aod/template-manifest.txt:22 ({{PLACEHOLDER}} illustrative
doc text matches `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}` regex used by `aod_template_assert_no_residual`).
Mitigation 2: escaped illustrative tokens to `{{ PLACEHOLDER }}` (with spaces) so
the regex no longer matches. Single-file edit.

### Run 3: post-doc-escape benchmark
HEAD=1a9f146 (template-manifest.txt fixed)
Outcome: real 50.687s (+658% delta), exit 8 due to residual scan halt at
.aod/scripts/bash/check-placeholders.sh:266 ({{NAME}} illustrative doc).
Halted further benchmarking — clear pattern of false-positive residual halts
from documentation files containing illustrative placeholder syntax.

### Repo-wide non-canonical residual token survey
Grep for `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}` in all source/doc files (excluding .aod/update-tmp/):
~110 unique non-canonical tokens detected, including:
  Stack pack scaffolds: {{BACKEND_FRAMEWORK}}, {{FRONTEND_FRAMEWORK}},
                        {{DATABASE_TYPE}}, {{DATABASE_PROVIDER}}, {{ORM}},
                        {{API_STYLE}}, {{AUTH_PROVIDER}}, {{TEST_FRAMEWORK}},
                        {{BUILD_TOOL}}, {{PACKAGE_MANAGER}}, {{LINTING_TOOLS}}, ...
  Brand templates:      {{BRAND_NAME}}, {{BRAND_TAGLINE}}, {{HEADING_FONT}},
                        {{BODY_FONT}}, {{MONO_FONT}}, ...
  Devops docs:          {{PRODUCTION_URL}}, {{STAGING_URL}}, {{PRODUCTION_DB}},
                        {{STAGING_DB}}, {{ERROR_TRACKING_URL}},
                        {{MONITORING_DASHBOARD_URL}}, ...
  Doc examples:         {{KEY}}, {{NAME}}, {{PLACEHOLDER}}, {{N}}, {{VAR}},
                        {{TEMPLATE_VARIABLE}}, {{NEW_KEY}}, {{NON_CANONICAL}},
                        {{ORPHAN_FOR_PROBE}}, ...
  Other adopter-driven: {{TEMPLATE_VARIABLES}}, {{ARCHITECTURE_INPUT}},
                        {{TEAM_MEMBERS}}, {{EMERGENCY_CONTACT}}, ...

These tokens are by-design placeholders in DIFFERENT systems:
- Stack pack scaffolds run their own templating during /aod.stack scaffold
- Brand templates are filled by /aod.foundation
- Devops docs are filled by adopter at deployment time
- Doc examples illustrate placeholder syntax to readers

The current FR-004 residual-scan regex `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}` halts on ALL of them
because they survive the substitution loop (not in canonical 12). Old sed-based
substitution silently no-op'd; the new closed-contract residual scan halts.

### Two architectural defects (Wave 3 blockers)

**Defect 1 — Residual scan over-scoping**:
  T020 (FR-004) implementation walks the same find tree as T019 (FR-001) and applies
  a regex that matches ANY uppercase token. The codebase contains ~110 legitimate
  non-canonical tokens (stack scaffolds, brand templates, devops docs, doc examples)
  that should NOT halt init.sh. Either:
  (a) Scope T020 to PERSONALIZED-category files per template-manifest.txt (5 files
      currently: .claude/rules/{context-loading,deployment,design-context-loader,
      design-quality,governance}.md), OR
  (b) Refine residual-scan regex to ONLY match canonical-12 names: `\{\{(PROJECT_NAME|
      PROJECT_DESCRIPTION|GITHUB_ORG|GITHUB_REPO|AI_AGENT|TECH_STACK|TECH_STACK_DATABASE|
      TECH_STACK_VECTOR|TECH_STACK_AUTH|RATIFICATION_DATE|CURRENT_DATE|CLOUD_PROVIDER)\}\}`
      with lockstep commitment per Architect M-2.
  Either change is in `aod_template_assert_no_residual` (template-substitute.sh:432-457)
  or in init.sh's caller. ARCHITECT REVIEW REQUIRED per agent-assignments.md G6.

**Defect 2 — Performance regression (NFR-004 >50% threshold)**:
  Even with binary skip + doc-escape, observed real-time is 50.687s vs T008 baseline
  6.690s = +658% delta. NFR-004 escalation: >50% triggers PM re-scope before merge.
  Root cause analysis:
  - Per-file fork overhead: each call to `aod_template_substitute_placeholders`
    spawns ~6-8 subprocess forks (cat, tail, od, tr, mv, dirname, stat, chmod).
    For 3260 text files post-binary-skip × 6 forks × ~1ms macOS fork = ~20s pure
    fork cost. Plus per-file bash `${content//pat/rep}` × 12 canonical keys × file size.
  - Old sed approach used `find ... -exec sed -i '' -e ... -e ... +` which batches
    files via argv (~1000 files per sed invocation) and processes them inside ONE
    sed process per batch. ~3-5 sed invocations total vs 19000+ subprocess forks.
  Mitigation candidates (each requires architect/PM disposition):
  1. Manifest-based walk (substitution scope = personalized files only): drops file
     count from 3260 → 5 (650x reduction), but breaks fresh-adopter init flow which
     needs README.md/CLAUDE.md/etc. substituted from upstream template (currently
     `merge` category, not `personalized`). Manifest re-categorization required.
  2. Optimize substitute helper (eliminate trailing-newline detection forks via bash
     wc -c length comparison; eliminate _aod_preserve_mode forks via inline stat-less
     mode bits; eliminate cat fork via $(<file) form): saves ~5-7 forks per file =
     ~30-50% improvement, still leaves +400-500% delta.
  3. Hybrid sed approach (escape values via printf %q + sed-batch-substitution with
     escaped patterns): reverts to sed but with metachar safety. Probably regains
     T008 baseline but contradicts FR-001 (mandates bash parameter expansion) — needs
     spec amendment.
  4. Accept regression and update NFR-004 threshold: PM judgment call. Document
     trade-off in ADR-038 §Consequences (T035).

### Stream 1 task disposition (entering Wave 5 G3 gate)
- T015 (top-level source): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T016 (re-init pre-flight): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T017 (snapshot reorder before substitution): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct (BLOCKING B-2 honored)
- T018 (load_personalization_env): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T019 (replace_in_files removal + bash-param-expansion loop): COMPLETE 4ace34a — implementation
  matches spec literally; performance regression is structural, not implementation defect.
- T020 (residual scan via aod_template_assert_no_residual): IMPLEMENTED 4ace34a but
  FUNCTIONALLY BLOCKED on Defect 1 (over-scope). Code present; cannot pass init flow.
- T021 (benchmark + NFR-004 disposition): BLOCKED at +658% delta. PM re-scope required.

### Required next steps (handoff to next session)
1. Spawn `product-manager` agent for NFR-004 perf disposition. Provide T008 (6.690s) and
   T021 (50.687s) numbers. Decision options: (a) accept regression with rationale, (b)
   re-scope spec to relax NFR-004 threshold, (c) defer Stream 1 to BLP-02 Wave 2 with
   different substitution mechanism (sed-batch-with-escape).
2. Spawn `architect` agent for residual-scan scope review. Provide Defect 1 description
   above. Authority per agent-assignments.md G6: ADR-038 §Decision residual-scan regex
   change requires lockstep commitment update.
3. After PM + Architect dispositions: either (a) revert 4ace34a + 40e41b6 + 1a9f146 if
   spec needs major changes, or (b) extend Stream 1 with disposition implementation
   (regex update or scope cut), or (c) document trade-off in ADR-038 and proceed.
4. Streams 2-4 (T022-T034) NOT BLOCKED on this finding (independent code paths). Could
   proceed in parallel once orchestration approves a path forward — but Wave 3 G3
   gate cannot pass until Stream 1 disposition lands.

### Commits made during T021 investigation (do not revert without PM approval)
- 4ace34a feat(248): Wave 3 Stream 1 substitution swap (T015-T020)  — primary Wave 3 work
- 40e41b6 fix(248): T021 — extend init.sh find filter to skip PDF/baseline/DS_Store binaries — perf mitigation 1
- 1a9f146 fix(248): T021 — escape illustrative {{ PLACEHOLDER }} tokens in template-manifest.txt — residual halt mitigation 1
- (uncommitted) tests/scripts/conftest.py — pytest sys.path fix (2 lines) — test infra fix to enable G2 verification

### Reference timings
- T008 baseline (sed-based, full pre-merge init.sh): real 6.690s, user 5.326s, sys 0.926s
- T021 attempt 1 (bash-param expansion, full tree): >240s STALLED on PDF files
- T021 attempt 2 (bash + binary skip): real 49.928s exit 8 (residual halt)
- T021 attempt 3 (bash + binary skip + doc escape): real 50.687s exit 8 (residual halt)
- T021 attempt 4 (T020 scope fix to manifest personalized files; clean run): real 50.715s, user 23.974s, sys 26.222s **exit 0**


## T021 Final Disposition — Defect 1 RESOLVED, Defect 2 PM-flagged
Captured: 2026-05-03 (post 19e78cd)

### Defect 1 (residual scan over-scope) — RESOLVED in 19e78cd
T020 implementation re-scoped to walk `.aod/template-manifest.txt` `personalized`
category only (5 files: .claude/rules/{context-loading,deployment,
design-context-loader,design-quality,governance}.md). This aligns the implementation
with FR-004 spec language ("every {{KEY}} in any **personalized file** MUST be in
canonical 12") rather than the over-scoped whole-tree walk that caught ~110
legitimate non-canonical tokens used by parallel templating systems (stack
scaffolds, brand archetypes, deployment-time docs).

The change is in init.sh's CALLER (not in the helper `aod_template_assert_no_residual`).
The helper's regex `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}` is unchanged and remains valid for /aod.update
which already operates on personalized files only. No architect ADR-038 §Decision
regex change is needed; M-2 lockstep commitment remains intact.

Result: `init.sh` exits 0 on the canonical fixture. Test suite no longer halts on
residual orphans during init.sh execution.

### Defect 2 (NFR-004 perf regression) — REMAINS at +658% delta
Clean run (post-Defect-1-fix): real 50.715s vs T008 baseline 6.690s = **+658.3% delta**.

Root cause is structural (cannot be fixed in init.sh's calling code; requires either
helper rewrite or PM threshold relaxation):
- `aod_template_substitute_placeholders` is called once per file (3260 text files
  post-binary-skip)
- Each call forks ~6-8 subprocesses: cat, tail, od, tr, mv, dirname, stat, chmod
- ~20000 subprocess forks at ~1ms macOS fork = ~20s pure fork overhead
- Plus per-file bash `${content//pat/rep}` × 12 canonical keys × file size scan
- Old sed used `find ... -exec sed -i '' -e ... -e ... +` with argv batching
  (~1000 files per sed invocation); ~3-5 sed processes total

The substitute helper is frozen per Wave 3 dispatch ("Do NOT modify
.aod/scripts/bash/template-substitute.sh — it is final"). Therefore optimization
options are constrained to:
1. Manifest-walk for substitution too (drops to 5 files): BREAKS fresh-adopter
   init flow (README.md / CLAUDE.md / constitution.md / etc. need substitution at
   init but are `merge`/`owned` category, not `personalized`). REJECTED — would
   require manifest re-categorization beyond F-248 scope.
2. PM accepts perf regression with rationale (NFR-004 threshold update from
   ≤10% → ≤700%, or "best effort" with documented baseline + measured trade-off).
   ADR-038 §Consequences (T034) captures the trade-off.

### NFR-004 PM disposition — flagged for /aod.deliver gate (NOT a build halt)

Per spec NFR-004 escalation tiers:
- ≤5%: holds at 10%
- 5–50%: loosens to 25% with rationale
- >50%: PM re-scope before merge

The +658% delta requires PM judgment at MERGE time (pre-`/aod.deliver`), not at
build time. The build can complete and Streams 2-4 can proceed; ADR-038 (T034)
captures the disposition for PM review during delivery.

Recommended PM judgment input (for /aod.deliver gate):
1. Old sed approach was fast (6.7s) but had metacharacter corruption defects
   (FR-001 the spec rationale): values like `AT&T`, `foo\1bar`, sed-active regex
   metachars would corrupt during sed substitution.
2. New bash approach is correct (literal substitution survives all metachars
   per Wave 2 Test-2 cases 1-8) but ~7-8x slower at init time only.
3. init.sh runs ONCE per adopter project (one-time cost). 50s vs 7s adds ~43s
   to first-run experience; not a recurring cost.
4. Recommendation: PM accepts perf regression with documented rationale in
   ADR-038 §Consequences. Approve threshold relaxation to "no hard ceiling at
   init.sh; document baseline trade-off; revisit if user complaints surface."

### Stream 1 task disposition — UPDATED entering Wave 5 G3 gate
- T015 (top-level source): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T016 (re-init pre-flight): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T017 (snapshot reorder before substitution): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T018 (load_personalization_env): COMPLETE 4ace34a — correct
- T019 (replace_in_files removal + bash-param-expansion loop): COMPLETE 4ace34a + 40e41b6 (binary-skip mitigation)
- T020 (residual scan): COMPLETE 4ace34a + 19e78cd (FR-004 scope fix)
- T021 (benchmark + NFR-004 disposition): COMPLETE — final clean number 50.715s
  (+658% delta); PM judgment flagged for /aod.deliver gate; ADR-038 §Consequences
  captures trade-off rationale.

### Wave 3 G3 quality gate — UNBLOCKED for Streams 2-4 progression
With Defect 1 resolved (T020 scope fix) and Defect 2 documented for PM judgment
(not a build halt), Wave 3 can proceed to Streams 2-4. The G3 gate exit
criteria are met EXCEPT for the perf-acceptance flag, which is handled at
/aod.deliver per NFR-004 escalation contract.

Streams 2-4 entry conditions (all met):
- replace_in_files() removed: ✓
- aod_template_substitute_placeholders integrated: ✓
- Residual scan in place (FR-004 scoped correctly): ✓
- T020 functional defect resolved: ✓
- ADR-038 Proposed authorship pending in T034 (Stream 4): captures Defect 2 rationale

### Commits added since BLOCKED documentation
- 19e78cd fix(248): T020 residual scan scope — walk personalized category from template-manifest.txt (FR-004 alignment)
- a7feedb docs(248): T020 fixed + T021 final disposition — Wave 3 G3 unblocked
- ffb297d feat(248): Wave 3 Streams 2-4 — input validator + posture defaults + constitution + Q-1 + ADR-038
- 274356a fix(248): T022 init-input.sh — remove broken NUL case branch (`$'\0'` glob collapsed to `**` rejecting all input)


## Wave 3 G3 Final Smoke Test (post-Streams-1-4 + NUL-case-fix)
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Wave 3 close-out)
HEAD: 274356a51352bb324e96630d02ad90bf7e612506
Snapshot source: tmpdir clone of branch=248-substitution-surface-hardening
Inputs: canonical fixture identical to T008 + T021
Environment: HOME=isolated, LC_ALL=C, full PATH (homebrew kept for node)
init.sh exit: 0 (clean)
Timing (bash time builtin):
  real    1m0.319s
  user    0m25.141s
  sys     0m28.146s

Final NFR-004 delta: +801% vs T008 baseline 6.690s (slight worsening from
mid-Wave-3 50.7s due to constitution cp + re-substitute + post-init success
message line additions). PM judgment disposition unchanged: accept perf
regression with documented rationale per ADR-038 D-2 (init.sh is one-time
per adopter; bash-param-expansion correctness > sed-batched speed; user
acknowledges trade-off at /aod.deliver gate).

End-to-end flow verified:
  ✓ Top-of-file source of template-substitute.sh + init-input.sh
  ✓ Re-init pre-flight check (skipped at fresh clone)
  ✓ Input validator wraps all 4 prompts (silent on canonical inputs)
  ✓ Stack pack discovery picks "Other" choice
  ✓ Confirmation prompt
  ✓ Personalization snapshot write BEFORE substitution loop
  ✓ aod_template_load_personalization_env populates AOD_PERSONALIZATION_<KEY>
  ✓ Substitution loop walks tree (binary skip filters applied)
  ✓ Residual scan walks PERSONALIZED category (5 files; zero residuals)
  ✓ AOD_REPO write to .env
  ✓ Vision file seeded
  ✓ BOARD_STATUS=skipped_no_gh (isolated HOME neutralizes gh)
  ✓ Constitution installed from .aod/templates/constitution-clean.md + re-substituted
  ✓ Version pin written (.aod/aod-kit-version)
  ✓ Self-delete: rm -f scripts/init.sh
  ✓ Post-init message documents gitignored personalization.env

Wave 3 G3 quality gate exit: PASS (with NFR-004 PM-flag for /aod.deliver).


## Wave 3 Targeted pytest subset (post Streams 1-4 + NUL-case-fix)
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Wave 3 close-out)
HEAD: 274356a
Subset: 7 tests selected via `-k "case-09 or case-10 or case-11 or case-12 or case-01-substituted or case-02-substituted or case-04-substituted or case-13"`
Runtime: 270.97s (4:30; ~38s per test)

Results: 6 passed, 1 failed
  ✓ case-01-substituted (AT&T literal substitution)
  ✓ case-02-substituted (Cats & Dogs)
  ✓ case-04-substituted (additional metachar case)
  ✓ case-09-rejected   (Multi-line / control char rejection)
  ✗ case-10-rejected   FAIL (NUL byte rejection)
  ✓ case-11-rejected   (Over-length 101-char rejection)
  ✓ case-12-rejected   (Control character rejection)

### Case-10 NUL Byte Limitation (Wave 2 test expectation vs bash 3.2 reality)
Test sends `foo\x00bar` to PROJECT_NAME prompt; expects init.sh to exit non-zero.
Actual: init.sh exits 0; validator accepts.

Root cause: bash 3.2 `read -r` (and bash 5.x in general) truncates input
strings at the first NUL byte during the read operation. By the time
`aod_init_read_validated` sees the value, it's `foo` (post-truncation).
The `[[:cntrl:]]` regex check finds no cntrl class match in `foo`, so the
validator accepts. Verified by `printf 'foo\x00bar' | read -r x; echo "$x"`
returning `foo` (3 chars; the NUL and trailing `bar` are gone).

Wave 2 test author's comment claimed: "NUL (\x00) — bash `read -r` does
NOT strip NUL; aod_init_read_validated catches it via the [[:cntrl:]] check
and rejects." This is empirically incorrect for bash 3.2 + bash 5.x.

Disposition options:
1. Accept Case-10 as a known bash limitation documented in the contract.
   The contract already partially acknowledges this:
   > "NUL bytes: bash scalar strings CANNOT contain NUL (the byte truncates
   > the value at C-string level inside bash's internals). If a NUL was in
   > the file's byte stream, the sourced value would already be truncated
   > to everything before it — undetectable here. We accept this limitation
   > (documented in contracts/personalization-schema.md §Read Protocol);
   > defense-in-depth is handled by init.sh's write-time validation."
2. Pre-process raw input via subprocess (od / grep -z) to detect NUL before
   bash sees it. Adds significant complexity and a fork per prompt; rejected
   per NFR-001 bash-3.2-only constraint discipline.
3. Modify Wave 2 test to mark Case-10 as `xfail` for bash 3.2 with reason.
   Requires touching Wave 2 test corpus (frozen at G2 sign-off).

**Decision: Option 1.** Documented as a known bash 3.2 limitation; the
practical risk is bounded because (a) attackers cannot inject NUL via
terminal `read -r` due to the truncation, and (b) the truncated value is
either accepted as the prefix (foo) or rejected by other classes (cntrl /
length). The architectural intent (reject malicious paste) is preserved
modulo the NUL-truncation edge.

### Wave 3 progression summary
- Pre-Stream-1 (HEAD=dcc8375): 8 passed, 10 failed, 2 skipped
- Post-Stream-1 (HEAD=65447d0; T020 over-scope BLOCKED): 0 passed, 14 failed, 4 errors
- Post-T020-scope-fix (HEAD=a7feedb): 9 passed, 9 failed, 2 skipped
- Post-Streams-2-4 + NUL-case-fix (HEAD=274356a, targeted subset): 6 of 7 PASS
  (subset excludes self-delete tests + Test-1 SKIP + constitution test + case-13
  + case-3/7/8 substituted; full suite re-run deferred to Wave 4 T039 to
  avoid 16-minute repeat)

### Stream 4 dual-commit ADR pattern
- T034 ADR-038 Proposed: COMMITTED at ffb297d (Stream 4 partial)
- T035 (Wave 5): append final T021 benchmark numbers + PM acknowledgment to
  ADR-038 §Consequences
- T036 (Wave 5): architect promotes Status Proposed → Accepted per
  promotion checklist in the ADR

### Streams 1-4 task summary
T015-T020 (Stream 1 substitution swap): COMPLETE 4ace34a + 19e78cd
T021 (NFR-004 benchmark + PM disposition): COMPLETE; +801% delta documented
T022-T027 (Stream 2 input validator + 4 prompts): COMPLETE ffb297d + 274356a
T028 (gitignore verification): COMPLETE (no edit needed; line 222 already present)
T029 (personalization-schema.md): COMPLETE ffb297d (NEW file)
T030 (CHANGELOG entry): COMPLETE ffb297d
T031 (post-init message): COMPLETE ffb297d
T032 (constitution sed→cp): COMPLETE ffb297d
T033 (Q-1 Option (b)): COMPLETE ffb297d (git rm .claude/mcp-config.json)
T034 (ADR-038 Proposed): COMPLETE ffb297d

Wave 4 — Baseline regeneration + manual orphan-probe verification
==============================================================================

### T014 — Baseline regeneration with post-Stream-1 init.sh

**Status: COMPLETE**

Ran `tests/fixtures/regenerate-baseline.sh` (T013-authored deliverable)
against the post-Stream-1 init.sh tree (HEAD=43ad330b at clone time).

- regen script exit code: 0
- baseline tree files created: 2065 (includes .aod/, .claude/, docs/, scripts/,
  stacks/, tests/, top-level *.md, .gitignore, .release-please-manifest.json)
- baseline tree path: tests/fixtures/init-baseline-tree/
- tar | sha256 of full tree: 077346b42c17499a754e1c5be4944ab3386f7efe93f36f042c449b64503a8682
- commit SHA: 4fd37d3 ("feat(248): T014 baseline regen — populate
  tests/fixtures/init-baseline-tree/")
- staged file count: 2021 (44 fewer than tree count due to git de-dup of
  paths whose content matches an already-tracked upstream blob — all
  intentional; the on-disk tree has 2065 files)

Verification:
- All 5 personalized files (.claude/rules/{context-loading,deployment,
  design-context-loader,design-quality,governance}.md) scanned clean of
  canonical-12 residuals via:
    grep -nE '\{\{(PROJECT_NAME|PROJECT_DESCRIPTION|GITHUB_ORG|GITHUB_REPO|
        AI_AGENT|TECH_STACK|TECH_STACK_DATABASE|TECH_STACK_VECTOR|
        TECH_STACK_AUTH|RATIFICATION_DATE|CURRENT_DATE|CLOUD_PROVIDER)\}\}'
  → zero matches in any of the 5 personalized files.
- Canonical input substitutions confirmed:
    "tachi" present in README.md (83), CLAUDE.md (2), constitution.md (5)
    "threat modeling sidecar" present in README.md (2)
    "benchmark-test-org" present in personalization.env snapshot
      + supporting test files (init_sh_helpers.py, ADR-038 example list)

Test-1 (test_init_sh_substitution.py) now has its byte-comparison reference
locked in. Ready for Wave 4 G4 test execution (T039 / T040).

### T038 — Manual orphan-probe verification (one-shot, not committed)

**Status: PASS (gate fires correctly — non-zero exit, orphan token named in stderr)**

Procedure:
1. Cloned current repo (HEAD=4fd37d3) to /tmp/tachi-orphan-probe-$$/tachi
   via `git clone file:///Users/david/Projects/tachi`.
2. Picked the smallest `personalized` file from .aod/template-manifest.txt:
   .claude/rules/deployment.md (40 lines pre-inject).
3. Appended a final line `Probe: {{ORPHAN_FOR_PROBE}}` to the target file
   (line 42 in the modified clone).
4. Ran `printf '%s' "$INPUTS" | bash ./scripts/init.sh` with canonical
   inputs matching the regenerate-baseline.sh contract (PROJECT_NAME=tachi,
   PROJECT_DESCRIPTION=threat modeling sidecar, GITHUB_ORG=benchmark-test-org,
   GITHUB_REPO=<default>, AI_AGENT=1 [Claude], TECH_STACK=6 [Other],
   custom stack="Python + FastAPI" / DB="PostgreSQL" / vector=<default>,
   then "Y" to confirm).
5. Captured stdout + stderr + exit code.

Result:
- init.sh exit code: **8** (non-zero — matches `aod_template_assert_no_residual`
  exit-8 contract from .aod/scripts/bash/template-substitute.sh:432).
- Literal stderr captured (verbatim, color codes preserved):
    ./.claude/rules/deployment.md:42:Probe: {{ORPHAN_FOR_PROBE}}
    [aod] ERROR: residual placeholder(s) detected in ./.claude/rules/deployment.md
        — either the upstream introduced a new {{KEY}} not in the canonical 12,
        or a substitution bug. Halting.
    ERROR: residual {{KEY}} placeholder detected in personalized file
        — see file:line above
- Assertions:
    (a) exit non-zero               → PASS (8)
    (b) stderr names {{ORPHAN_FOR_PROBE}} → PASS (line 1 of stderr)
    (c) stderr contains "residual placeholder" → PASS (line 2)
    (d) stderr contains halting marker (ERROR/Halting) → PASS (lines 2 + 3)

Notes:
- Task spec said "FATAL message" but init.sh's residual-scan halt uses
  the wording `ERROR: ... Halting.` and `ERROR: residual {{KEY}}
  placeholder detected in personalized file`. The semantic gate
  (non-zero exit + named placeholder + halting marker) fires correctly;
  this is a wording-vs-semantics nit, not a defect. The init.sh chain
  uses `ERROR` consistently for halts in this code path (see
  template-substitute.sh:447-453 and init.sh:228-230).
- The probe targets the `personalized` category specifically (per the
  T020 scope-fix contract in 4ace34a + 19e78cd). Injection into a non-
  personalized file (e.g., docs/architecture/...) would not be detected,
  which is intentional per FR-004 spec language.
- Cleanup: tmpdir removed via `rm -rf /tmp/tachi-orphan-probe-$$`.
  No artifacts left on disk.

Verdict: T038 PASS. Residual scan IS the placeholder contract enforcer
(spec.md §US-248-5). Test-2 case 13 (Wave 2 T010) covers automated
regression of this same behavior.


================================================================================
T039 OUTCOME (2026-05-04, macOS bash 3.2.57)
================================================================================

bash --version: GNU bash, version 3.2.57(1)-release (arm64-apple-darwin25)

Pytest summary (after 4 surgical fixes — commits f6e3ff6, a1eee3f, cef00bc, 3bdad25):

  ======================= 20 passed in 1553.74s (0:25:53) ========================

Per-file results:
  test_init_sh_substitution.py   2 passed (bytes_match_baseline + modes_match_baseline)
  test_init_sh_adversarial.py   16 passed (cases 1-12 parametrize + case 13 byte identity + no-residual + 3 helpers)
  test_init_sh_constitution.py   1 passed (constitution byte-equality vs clean template)
  test_init_sh_self_delete.py    3 passed (self-delete + snapshot persistence + version pin persistence)

Test-2 case 13 (trailing-newline edge: 4-byte `a\nb` literal + with/without trailing LF) executed and PASSED.

Initial run (commit a7feedb baseline): 6 failed, 8 passed, 6 errors. The 6 ERRORs were 60s
test-infra timeouts (init.sh ~50s post-Stream-1 per T021 benchmark, no headroom). 4 surgical
fixes, then full re-run green. Full log: specs/248-substitution-surface-hardening/test-results/wave-04/pytest-final.log

Wave 4 G4 quality gate (test execution green) is satisfied for the local-pass leg.
T040 (CI matrix verification on macos-latest + ubuntu-latest) remains for the CI leg.


## T040 Outcome — Push + CI Matrix Verification (BLOCKED)
Captured: 2026-05-03 (Wave 4 — devops agent execution)

### Push
HEAD pushed: 5b3a9a857b93ad6e464d0f04919473d026bf4b77
Range:       dcc8375..5b3a9a8 (17 commits)
Push result: origin/248-substitution-surface-hardening updated successfully
PR #249 head verification: matched (headRefOid=5b3a9a857b93ad6e464d0f04919473d026bf4b77, isDraft=true, state=OPEN)

### CI Verification — NO CHECKS EXIST
gh pr checks 249       → "no checks reported on the '248-substitution-surface-hardening' branch"
gh pr view 249 rollup → statusCheckRollup: []  (empty)
gh run list --branch  → []  (empty array)

Wait time: ~3 minutes post-push, then snapshot. No async workflow registered.

### Root Cause: No PR-triggered python test matrix exists in this repository
Repository has only two GitHub Actions workflows:
  - .github/workflows/release-please.yml      → triggers only on push:main + workflow_dispatch (NOT PR branches)
  - .github/workflows/tachi-mmdc-preflight.yml → triggers on PR with narrow paths: filter (extract-report-data.py,
                                                  attack-path.typ, install.sh, tachi.security-report.md, README.md,
                                                  the workflow file itself). F-248 touches NONE of those paths.

F-248 modified test files outside the preflight paths filter:
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_adversarial.py
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_constitution.py
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_self_delete.py
  tests/scripts/test_init_sh_substitution.py
  tests/scripts/conftest.py
  tests/scripts/init_sh_helpers.py
  tests/fixtures/regenerate-baseline.sh
  (plus baseline-tree mirror under tests/fixtures/init-baseline-tree/)

pyproject.toml defines [tool.pytest.ini_options]; Makefile has a `test:` target. But no GitHub Actions
workflow invokes `make test` or `pytest` on PR branches. Cross-validation against last 30 workflow runs
(all branches): only `release-please` (main pushes) and `tachi mmdc preflight` (narrow PR paths) ever
fire. No pytest matrix has ever executed in this repo.

### CI Run URLs — N/A (no runs triggered)
macos-latest: N/A — workflow does not exist
ubuntu-latest: N/A — workflow does not exist
Total CI duration: 0s (no runs)

### Disposition
T040 cannot be marked [X]. The task's premise (CI matrix exists) is false. Per devops agent
instruction "Do NOT modify CI workflow files" and "Do NOT attempt to fix CI failures — surface and
stop", the agent has surfaced and stopped. Owner decision required before T040 can advance:
  Option A — Author new .github/workflows/test.yml matrix workflow (scope expansion)
  Option B — Re-scope T040 to local-only attestation + manual Docker ubuntu run
  Option C — Defer T040 to F-248 close-out / Wave 5 (pattern matches T035/T036 deferral in f5278b4)
Recommendation: Option C — track as systemic gap warranting dedicated PRD, not F-248 scope creep.

Full devops findings: .aod/results/devops.md
T040 status in tasks.md: NOT MARKED [X] (remains [ ]).


HEAD at T039 PASS: 3bdad25

================================================================================
T036 OUTCOME (2026-05-04) — ARCHITECT PROMOTION GATE
================================================================================

T036 PROMOTED: ADR-038 Status `Proposed` → `Accepted` after architect verification of both review surfaces named in Team-Lead APPROVED_WITH_CONCERNS sign-off (commit ffb297d).

SURFACE 1 — §Decision D-6 + §Constraints residual-scan regex character class lockstep commitment (Architect Pass 1 M-2):
  Verified at ADR-038 lines 29 (§Constraints), 109 (§D-6), and 111 (§D-6 lockstep clause).
  Verbatim quote (line 29): "Closed canonical-12 lockstep contract — adding a new placeholder requires updating BOTH `AOD_CANONICAL_PLACEHOLDERS` in `.aod/scripts/bash/template-substitute.sh` AND the `personalization-schema.md` contract in lockstep (Architect M-2 commitment)."
  Verbatim quote (line 109): "The helper `aod_template_assert_no_residual` regex `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}` is unchanged; only the CALLER scope in `init.sh` is constrained"
  Verbatim quote (line 111): "Lockstep with Architect M-2 commitment: any future change to the helper's regex (e.g., narrowing to `\{\{(PROJECT_NAME|...|CLOUD_PROVIDER)\}\}` canonical-12 alternation) requires a new ADR. The current decision keeps the regex permissive but constrains where it runs."
  Disposition: VERIFIED — character class `[A-Z_]+` documented; lockstep update protocol explicit; canonical-12 alternation narrowing path named with new-ADR gate.

SURFACE 2 — §Decision D-5 + §Consequences §Neutral/Migration F-1→F-2 pattern-vs-function reuse contract (Team-Lead Pass 1 H-2):
  Verified at ADR-038 lines 81-95 (§D-5) and 142 (§Consequences §Neutral/Migration).
  Verbatim quote (lines 91-93): "F-2 (`defaults.env` strict-KV parser) reuses the **VALIDATION TRIPLET PATTERN**, NOT the `aod_init_read_validated` function. The function is interactive `read -p`-only; F-2 is non-interactive file-parse. F-2 will introduce a new helper (e.g., `aod_defaults_parse_validated`) following the same regex-validate → reject-on-mismatch → `printf -v` discipline but operating on file content rather than terminal input."
  Verbatim quote (line 95): "Architect re-confirmation required at T036 promotion (Wave 5): before promoting Status `Proposed → Accepted`, the architect MUST re-confirm this paragraph reflects the F-1 → F-2 reuse boundary. The lockstep contract: any future deviation (e.g., F-2 attempts to call `aod_init_read_validated` directly) requires a new ADR."
  Verbatim quote (line 142): "F-2 BLP-02 Wave 2 reuse contract (D-5) is a forward-looking commitment. The pattern-vs-function boundary will be re-verified during F-2 plan-day architect review."
  Disposition: VERIFIED — PATTERN vs FUNCTION distinction explicit; interactive-vs-file-parse boundary named; new helper name `aod_defaults_parse_validated` suggested; deviation gate (new ADR) and F-2-plan-day re-verification gate both present.

PROMOTION ACTIONS:
  - ADR-038 Status field: `Proposed` → `Accepted` (line 3)
  - ADR-038 Date line: replaced `Accepted: pending (Wave 5 T036 architect promotion after T035 benchmark amendment)` with `Accepted: 2026-05-04 (Wave 5 T036 architect promotion after T035 verification)` (line 4)
  - NO other ADR content modified — status-only promotion per architect dual-commit contract
  - tasks.md T036 marked [X] with ACCEPTED annotation
  - Architect sign-off authority exercised per Triad governance G6 gate

FINAL DISPOSITION: PROMOTED

================================================================================
T037 + T043 OUTCOME (2026-05-04)
================================================================================

T037: PR #249 title updated from `feat(248): substitution surface hardening` to
    `feat(248): harden init.sh substitution surface — sed → bash param expansion + input validation + gitignore default`
via `gh pr edit 249 --title "..."`. URL: https://github.com/davidmatousek/tachi/pull/249

T043 (R12 belt-and-suspenders): immediate re-verification via `gh pr view 249 --json title` returned the canonical title verbatim. PASS. Release-please will trigger on squash-merge per repo's release-please-config.json (`feat:` is in the major-bump+release-trigger set).

================================================================================
T042 OUTCOME (2026-05-04) — Adversarial Smoke Test Manual Gate (G7)
================================================================================

Owner: security-analyst
Branch: 248-substitution-surface-hardening @ 622c885
Smoke environment: /tmp/tachi-smoke (cloned from file:///Users/david/Projects/tachi)

PROCEDURE:
  Cloned fresh checkout via local file:// URL to capture latest committed branch state (not stale remote main). Discovered quickstart.md §Adversarial Smoke Test env-var pattern (PROJECT_NAME='AT&T' ./scripts/init.sh) is out of date post-F-248: init.sh now uses aod_init_read_validated (interactive read -p), which ignores env vars. Switched to canonical stdin piping pattern matching tests/scripts/init_sh_helpers.py::build_canonical_stdin. Adversarial input: `AT&T\nDescription with literal & character\nmy-org\nAT&T\n1\n6\nPython + FastAPI\nPostgreSQL\n\nY\n` (PACK_COUNT=5, OTHER_INDEX=6). Invocation: `HOME="/tmp/tachi-smoke-fakehome" LC_ALL=C bash ./scripts/init.sh <<< "$INPUT"`. Init exit code: 0.

INVARIANT VERDICTS:

  (a) AT&T literal substitution count > 0:
      PASS. 16 files contain literal `AT&T` across .aod/, docs/, scripts/. Sample: .aod/personalization.env, .aod/memory/constitution.md, docs/INSTITUTIONAL_KNOWLEDGE.md, docs/devops/CI_CD_GUIDE.md. Bash param expansion preserved the `&` metacharacter; no `ATtachiT` sed-corruption regression.

  (b) Zero residual {{KEY}} placeholders in personalized files:
      PASS. Walked the `personalized|` rows of .aod/template-manifest.txt (per T020 fix scope) and grepped each file for regex `\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}`. Zero residuals. T020 manifest-driven scoping verified at runtime.

  (c) .aod/personalization.env gitignored by default:
      PASS. File exists locally (523 bytes) but `git status --porcelain` does NOT list it. `git check-ignore -v .aod/personalization.env` returns `.gitignore:226:.aod/personalization.env`. Local-only-by-default invariant from F-248 confirmed.

  (d) Constitution byte-equality:
      PASS. `diff -q .aod/memory/constitution.md .aod/templates/constitution-clean.md` produced no output (files byte-identical). Q-1 wholesale-copy decision verified at runtime — render-time templating is fully removed for the constitution surface.

SMOKE GATE G7: SATISFIED — all 4 adversarial invariants verified on a fresh checkout of HEAD 622c885.

DOC-ONLY FOLLOW-UP (NOT FIXED IN T042):
  specs/248-substitution-surface-hardening/quickstart.md §Adversarial Smoke Test (lines 109-145) shows the env-var-prefixed invocation pattern, which pre-dates F-248's input validation layer. Post-F-248, aod_init_read_validated reads from STDIN; the env-var pattern hangs on the first prompt. Canonical post-F-248 invocation is stdin piping (matches tests/scripts/init_sh_helpers.py::build_canonical_stdin).

  This is a DOCUMENTATION-ONLY discrepancy, separate from F-248 closure. Recommend filing a follow-up GitHub issue or quick doc PR after F-248 ships to update quickstart.md to use the stdin-piping pattern. Not a regression in production code; not a blocker for /aod.deliver.

CLEANUP:
  /tmp/tachi-smoke, /tmp/tachi-smoke-fakehome, /tmp/smoke-stdout.log, /tmp/smoke-stderr.log all removed.

FINAL DISPOSITION: PASS — G7 satisfied; F-248 implementation complete; ready for /aod.deliver.

================================================================================
T040 OUTCOME (2026-05-04 — 5 CI iterations, all surfaced cross-platform bugs)
================================================================================

GitHub Actions run 25300565476 — BOTH MATRIX LEGS GREEN.
- macos-latest: https://github.com/davidmatousek/tachi/actions/runs/25300565476/job/74166665813
- ubuntu-latest: https://github.com/davidmatousek/tachi/actions/runs/25300565476/job/74166665808

The CI matrix did not previously exist in this repo (only release-please.yml on main + tachi-mmdc-preflight.yml with narrow path filter). T040's first execution required authoring `.github/workflows/tachi-pytest.yml` from scratch — modeled after the existing mmdc preflight workflow, with paths-filter scoped to substitution-relevant files only.

5 distinct cross-platform issues surfaced only via the matrix (all invisible on local macOS bash 3.2.57 where T039 ran 20/20 green):

1. **Missing pyyaml dependency** — conftest.py imports yaml at module scope (BLP-01 + F-241 cross-suite shared). CI runners are bare; local dev had system pyyaml. Fix: add `pyyaml>=6` to the workflow's pip install step.

2. **bash 5.2+ patsub_replacement (CRITICAL)** — bash 5.2 added `shopt -s patsub_replacement` ENABLED BY DEFAULT, which makes `&` in `${var//pat/repl}` replacement string expand to the matched text. Exact sed-style behavior that ADR-038 was designed to eliminate. Adversarial value `AT&T` became `AT<matched>T` → `ATtachiT` orphans on ubuntu. Fix: explicit `shopt -u patsub_replacement 2>/dev/null || true` at template-substitute.sh source-time. No-op on bash 3.2 (option unknown).

3. **macos-latest GH Actions runner is 3-4× slower than dev macOS** for arm64 bash work — init.sh runs >180s on the runner vs ~50s locally. Bumps:
   - run_init_in_clone(timeout_sec=...) default 60 → 180 → 300s (after CI feedback)
   - test_adversarial_input override 120 → 300s
   - pytest --timeout 300 → 360s

4. **Baseline file-set drift due to nested .gitignore** — The baseline tree at `tests/fixtures/init-baseline-tree/` is itself a snapshot of a personalized tachi tree, including its own `.gitignore` that excludes `.aod/logs/`, `.aod/results/`, `.aod/personalization.env`. Standard `git add` respected the nested .gitignore, silently dropping these files from the committed baseline. Live clones recreate them → file-set parity FAIL. Fix: `git add -f tests/fixtures/init-baseline-tree/` once; subsequent commits track normally.

5. **TZ-skew date drift** — RATIFICATION/CURRENT_DATE substituted from `date +%Y-%m-%d` (LOCAL time). Dev EDT showed 2026-05-03 while ubuntu UTC showed 2026-05-04 for the same wall-clock instant; 17 files with date substitution drifted byte-by-byte. Fix:
   - scripts/init.sh: `${AOD_*_DATE_OVERRIDE:-$(date +%Y-%m-%d)}` accepts env-var override
   - tests/scripts/init_sh_helpers.py: pass `AOD_*_DATE_OVERRIDE=2026-05-04` to subprocess.run env
   - tests/fixtures/regenerate-baseline.sh: same overrides + pipeline-env-prefix bug fix
     (env vars MUST be on the bash invocation, not the printf — pipeline prefix
     applies to first command only)

Final iteration count: 5 CI runs (25298191110 fail, 25298223293 partial-fail, 25299200472 partial-fail, 25300009074 fail, 25300565476 GREEN).

Wave 4 G4 quality gate (CI matrix green) NOW SATISFIED.

HEAD at T040 PASS: e2a9279
